  • Method Details

    • beneficiaryPercentage

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder beneficiaryPercentage(int beneficiaryPercentage)
      An integer conveying the percentage of interest the related Beneficiary has in the account if the owner(s) become deceased; The sum of all beneficiary percentages must equal "100"

    • birthDate

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder birthDate(DateCreate birthDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • birthDate

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder birthDate(Optional<? extends DateCreate> birthDate)
      Represents a whole or partial calendar date, such as a birthday. The time of day and time zone are either specified elsewhere or are insignificant. The date is relative to the Gregorian Calendar. This can represent one of the following:

      * A full date, with non-zero year, month, and day values * A month and day value, with a zero year, such as an anniversary * A year on its own, with zero month and day values * A year and month value, with a zero day, such as a credit card expiration date

      Related types are [google.type.TimeOfDay][google.type.TimeOfDay] and `google.protobuf.Timestamp`.

    • mailingAddress

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder mailingAddress(PostalAddressCreate mailingAddress)
      Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

      In typical usage an address would be created via user input or from importing existing data, depending on the type of process.

      Advice on address input / editing: - Use an i18n-ready address widget such as https://github.com/google/libaddressinput) - Users should not be presented with UI elements for input or editing of fields outside countries where that field is used.

      For more guidance on how to use this schema, please see: https://support.google.com/business/answer/6397478

    • mailingAddress

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder mailingAddress(Optional<? extends PostalAddressCreate> mailingAddress)
      Represents a postal address, e.g. for postal delivery or payments addresses. Given a postal address, a postal service can deliver items to a premise, P.O. Box or similar. It is not intended to model geographical locations (roads, towns, mountains).

      In typical usage an address would be created via user input or from importing existing data, depending on the type of process.

      Advice on address input / editing: - Use an i18n-ready address widget such as https://github.com/google/libaddressinput) - Users should not be presented with UI elements for input or editing of fields outside countries where that field is used.

      For more guidance on how to use this schema, please see: https://support.google.com/business/answer/6397478

    • middleNames

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder middleNames(String middleNames)
      Non-primary names representing a natural person; Name attributed to a person other than "Given" and "Family" names.

    • middleNames

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder middleNames(Optional<String> middleNames)
      Non-primary names representing a natural person; Name attributed to a person other than "Given" and "Family" names.

    • phoneNumber

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder phoneNumber(PhoneNumberCreate phoneNumber)
      An object representing a phone number, suitable as an API wire format.

      This representation:

      - should not be used for locale-specific formatting of a phone number, such as "+1 (650) 253-0000 ext. 123"

      - is not designed for efficient storage - may not be suitable for dialing - specialized libraries (see references) should be used to parse the number for that purpose

      To do something meaningful with this number, such as format it for various use-cases, convert it to an `i18n.phonenumbers.PhoneNumber` object first.

      For instance, in Java this would be:

      com.google.type.PhoneNumber wireProto = com.google.type.PhoneNumber.newBuilder().build(); com.google.i18n.phonenumbers.Phonenumber.PhoneNumber phoneNumber = PhoneNumberUtil.getInstance().parse(wireProto.getE164Number(), "ZZ"); if (!wireProto.getExtension().isEmpty()) { phoneNumber.setExtension(wireProto.getExtension()); }

      Reference(s): - https://github.com/google/libphonenumber

    • phoneNumber

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder phoneNumber(Optional<? extends PhoneNumberCreate> phoneNumber)
      An object representing a phone number, suitable as an API wire format.

      This representation:

      - should not be used for locale-specific formatting of a phone number, such as "+1 (650) 253-0000 ext. 123"

      - is not designed for efficient storage - may not be suitable for dialing - specialized libraries (see references) should be used to parse the number for that purpose

      To do something meaningful with this number, such as format it for various use-cases, convert it to an `i18n.phonenumbers.PhoneNumber` object first.

      For instance, in Java this would be:

      com.google.type.PhoneNumber wireProto = com.google.type.PhoneNumber.newBuilder().build(); com.google.i18n.phonenumbers.Phonenumber.PhoneNumber phoneNumber = PhoneNumberUtil.getInstance().parse(wireProto.getE164Number(), "ZZ"); if (!wireProto.getExtension().isEmpty()) { phoneNumber.setExtension(wireProto.getExtension()); }

      Reference(s): - https://github.com/google/libphonenumber

    • relationType

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder relationType(BeneficiaryCreateRelationType relationType)
      The relationship of the beneficiary to the account owner

    • taxId

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder taxId(String taxId)
      The full U.S. tax ID for a related person; Tax ID is required if birth date is not provided.

    • taxId

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder taxId(Optional<String> taxId)
      The full U.S. tax ID for a related person; Tax ID is required if birth date is not provided.

    • taxIdType

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder taxIdType(BeneficiaryCreateTaxIdType taxIdType)
      The nature of the U.S. Tax ID indicated in the related tax_id field; Examples include ITIN, SSN, EIN. Tax id type is required if birth date is not provided.

    • taxIdType

      public BeneficiaryCreate.Builder taxIdType(Optional<? extends BeneficiaryCreateTaxIdType> taxIdType)
      The nature of the U.S. Tax ID indicated in the related tax_id field; Examples include ITIN, SSN, EIN. Tax id type is required if birth date is not provided.

    • build

      public BeneficiaryCreate build()
